optimized_instructions:
Extract one structured specialty-coffee product record from the supplied raw ecommerce page text.

First identify the active/main product page, not navigation, footer, SEO/meta tags, breadcrumbs, recommendations, archive listings, or unrelated embedded variant blocks. Use only evidence in the page text; never use outside knowledge. Preserve the page’s original wording and language for human-facing coffee attributes.

Classify page_type as coffee_product, coffee_equipment, other_product, or not_a_product_page. If it is not a roasted coffee product for sale, set coffee/price fields to null, unknown, or false as appropriate and extraction_quality='poor'. Set is_specialty_coffee true only for coffee_product pages that show an origin plus at least one specialty cue such as process, variety, farm/producer, roast/harvest, or detailed tasting notes.

Extract coffee fields for the active product: coffee_name, roaster, roaster_location, roaster_country, origin_country, origin_region, producer_or_farm, process_method, variety, altitude, roast_level, harvest_period, sensory_text, display_tasting_notes, producer_text, and booleans is_blend, is_espresso, is_decaf, is_coferment_or_infused. Use null for absent human-facing fields and 'unknown' only for absent model labels such as roaster_country or unknown process/variety arrays. Do not guess origin, process, variety, price, currency, or package size.

Field rules: coffee_name is the product name; roaster is the selling roaster. origin_country is the producing country; use 'blend_multi_origin' only when multiple producing countries are clearly present. origin_region may be a region/province/farm area or 'multi_origin'. process_method and variety are arrays of exact stated terms, not normalized; use ['unknown'] only if absent. roast_level, harvest_period, and altitude should match the page wording when present. producer_or_farm may include farm, estate, producer, cooperative, mill, washing station, family, or smallholder group. is_blend is true only for clearly labeled blends or multiple origins. is_decaf is true for decaf/decaffeinated/Swiss Water/etc. is_coferment_or_infused is true only when additives such as fruit, botanicals, flavorings, or inoculated/infused ingredients are introduced, not for plain anaerobic/natural fermentation. is_espresso is true only when espresso is an explicit intended or endorsed use for this active product, not from unrelated products or incidental site text.

For sensory_text, use verbatim product tasting/cup/flavor/aroma/body/acidity/finish text, preferring dedicated tasting notes over generic marketing. display_tasting_notes should be a concise comma-separated list of note names only, usually 3-7 items, in the original language. producer_text should be verbatim origin/producer/process/lot/sourcing background prose.

Extract price for the selected/default active-product variant. If a selected_by_url variant exists, use it. Otherwise use the variant marked default_for_inference; if none is marked, choose the visible/default option or the package closest to 10 oz / 283.5g. Ignore variants belonging to recommendations or other products. Capture listed_price, listed_currency, original_listed_price/currency when applicable, bag_size_value, bag_size_unit, package_grams, bags_count, price_type, availability, price_100g_usd, and assumptions. Prefer one-time price over subscription when both exist; otherwise mark subscription or membership if required. availability is in_stock, sold_out, preorder, or unknown. Convert sizes using 1 oz=28.3495231g and 1 lb=453.59237g; for samplers/multipacks, package_grams is total grams and bags_count is the number of bags. Fill price_100g_usd only when price is USD and package_grams is known. Record assumptions such as inferred currency from a symbol or default variant selection.

Return short exact source_snippets for important fields such as name, origin, process, variety, price, bag_size, tasting_notes, producer, altitude, and roast. Also return a quality report with extraction_quality: 'good' when origin plus several production/tasting fields and package price are present; 'partial' when usable but important fields are missing; 'poor' for non-coffee pages or mostly absent evidence. missing_fields should list important absent inference fields, and warnings should note ambiguity, conflicting product blocks, multiple prices/variants, subscription-only pricing, unavailable products, weak evidence, or ignored unrelated content.
